title: test_modes.py crashes on Windows with UnicodeDecodeError: open(PI_AGENT_PATH, 'r') missing encoding='utf-8'
source: Claude Fable audit 2026-07-06 (discovered while re-verifying the full suite after this session's changes)
current_state: testing/test_modes.py:71 and :98 call 'open(PI_AGENT_PATH, \'r\')' with no explicit encoding. On Windows, open() without encoding= defaults to the process locale codepage (cp1252 here), not UTF-8. pi_agent.py contains legitimate UTF-8 characters (em-dashes in comments/docstrings, consistent with the rest of the codebase's style). Once pi_agent.py grew (or was edited) enough to place a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence at a position cp1252 can't decode, both tests crash with UnicodeDecodeError: 'charmap' codec can't decode byte 0x90 in position 35183. Line 47 in the SAME file already does this correctly: 'open(p, \'r\', encoding=\'utf-8\')' - the bug is isolated to two call sites that never got the same treatment.
target_state: Both open() calls (lines 71, 98) pass encoding='utf-8', matching line 47's existing correct pattern. Tests pass regardless of which locale codepage the CI/dev machine defaults to.
migration_plan: 3. Rerun: python -m pytest testing/test_modes.py -q - both previously-failing tests must pass.
4. python scripts/verify.py PASS; append solutions/SOLUTIONS.jsonl; close ticket.
risk_notes: Purely additive (encoding= keyword), zero behavior change on any platform where the default locale already happens to be UTF-8 (e.g. most Linux CI runners) - this is why the bug was Windows-only and easy to miss.
